preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context for trial users: reaffirms what they already know about the product and frames the page around upgrading, making the transition from trial to paid feel like a natural next step.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Validators need proof before committing. Presenting concrete metrics, usage data, or case study results early satisfies their need for verification and addresses skepticism before the pricing ask.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Reinforces the evidence with trust signals — customer logos, testimonials, or industry recognition. For Validators, stacking social proof on top of hard data creates the confidence threshold required to move toward a purchase decision.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: With proof and trust established, the plan comparison and pricing framing gives trial users a clear, rational choice architecture. Conversion-stage users are ready for this — they need clarity on what they get, not more persuasion.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Immediately follows Decision per rules. A direct, high-commitment CTA — 'Upgrade Now' or 'Start Your Plan' — capitalizes on the validated confidence built by Evidence and Credibility beats.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Provides secondary paths for trial users not yet ready: links to talk to sales, extend trial, or revisit feature documentation — ensuring no visitor is lost entirely.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages — never suppress it.

Validator variant includes description and longer feature list, which aligns with the Validator archetype's need for detailed information to make conversion decisions. Use highlighted variant for recommended plan to guide decision-making.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Everything you used in the trial — resolution engine, IA planner, multi-variant generation, palette map — plus lifetime access, no subscription.
features: Semantic resolution engine — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, context mode
IA planner: brief to full page architecture in seconds
Multi-variant generation — Mover, Validator, Explorer in parallel
Token dashboard with editable palette map and preset switching
Lifetime access — no subscription, no runtime dependency

page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). This addresses validator archetype's need for reassurance and reduces friction in the conversion funnel.

slots.headline: You've seen it work. Now own it.
slots.subhead: One payment. No subscription. The intelligence layer lives in your codebase — not a vendor dashboard you depend on indefinitely.
slots.trust_signal: Lifetime access. No recurring fees. Fork it, extend it, make it yours.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. This is critical for conversion stage funnel optimization.
